context: This chapter describes how sins committed in previous lives manifest as visible signs in subsequent births. Various sinful acts lead to rebirth as animals, insects, or humans afflicted with deformities and diseases, illustrating the inescapable law of karma.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
भगवन् सर्वधर्मज्ञ सर्वशास्त्रविशारद ।
पापिनां लक्षणं ब्रूहि येन ज्ञायन्ति पापिनः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuḍa uvāca —
Bhagavan sarva-dharmajña sarva-śāstra-viśārada |
pāpināṃ lakṣaṇaṃ brūhi yena jñāyanti pāpinaḥ ||
verses.translation: Garuda said: O Lord, knower of all dharma, master of all scriptures, tell me the signs of sinners by which they may be recognized.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
श्रृणु तार्क्ष्य प्रवक्ष्यामि पापिनां लक्षणं शुभम् ।
येन ज्ञात्वा नरो लोके पापकर्म परित्यजेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
śṛṇu Tārkṣya pravakṣyāmi pāpināṃ lakṣaṇaṃ śubham |
yena jñātvā naro loke pāpa-karma parityajet ||
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Listen, O Tarksya, I shall describe the signs of sinners. Knowing these, a man in this world should abandon sinful deeds.

verses.hindi:
ब्रह्महत्यां करोति यः पूर्वजन्मनि मानवः ।
स जायते ह्यगले रोगी यक्ष्मग्रस्तश्च जायते ॥
verses.transliteration:
brahma-hatyāṃ karoti yaḥ pūrva-janmani mānavaḥ |
sa jāyate hy agale rogī yakṣma-grastaś ca jāyate ||
verses.translation: He who commits the killing of a Brahmana in a previous birth is born with diseases of the throat and is afflicted with consumption.

verses.hindi:
गोहत्यां कुरुते यस्तु स जायते विकलाङ्गकः ।
पङ्गुः कुब्जोऽथवा वामनो जायते नात्र संशयः ॥
verses.transliteration:
go-hatyāṃ kurute yas tu sa jāyate vikalāṅgakaḥ |
paṅguḥ kubjo'thavā vāmano jāyate nātra saṃśayaḥ ||
verses.translation: He who kills a cow is born with deformed limbs — lame, hunchbacked, or dwarfed. There is no doubt about this.

verses.hindi:
स्त्रीहत्यां कुरुते यस्तु भ्रूणहत्यां करोति च ।
स जायते महारोगी कुष्ठी चैव न संशयः ॥
verses.transliteration:
strī-hatyāṃ kurute yas tu bhrūṇa-hatyāṃ karoti ca |
sa jāyate mahā-rogī kuṣṭhī caiva na saṃśayaḥ ||
verses.translation: He who kills a woman or destroys an embryo is born with severe diseases and becomes a leper, without doubt.

verses.hindi:
परदारान् नरो गत्वा जायते वृषलात्मजः ।
नपुंसकोऽथवा क्लीबो जायते नात्र संशयः ॥
verses.transliteration:
para-dārān naro gatvā jāyate vṛṣalātmajaḥ |
napuṃsako'thavā klībo jāyate nātra saṃśayaḥ ||
verses.translation: A man who goes to another's wife is born as an outcaste's son, or is born impotent or effeminate — there is no doubt.

verses.hindi:
सुवर्णस्तेयकृत् पापी नखरोगी प्रजायते ।
दुर्गन्धी पूतिवदनो जायते धान्यहारकः ॥
verses.transliteration:
suvarṇa-steya-kṛt pāpī nakha-rogī prajāyate |
durgandhī pūti-vadano jāyate dhānya-hārakaḥ ||
verses.translation: The sinner who steals gold is born with diseased nails. He who steals grain is born with a foul-smelling mouth.

verses.hindi:
अन्नस्तेयं नरः कृत्वा जायतेऽजीर्णरोगवान् ।
वस्त्रापहारकश्चैव श्वित्री भवति मानवः ॥
verses.transliteration:
anna-steyaṃ naraḥ kṛtvā jāyate'jīrṇa-rogavān |
vastrāpahārakaś caiva śvitrī bhavati mānavaḥ ||
verses.translation: A man who steals food is born with chronic indigestion. He who steals garments is born with white leprosy.

verses.hindi:
विषदाता भुजङ्गः स्यात् छागो मांसस्य भक्षकः ।
मधुहर्ता भवेन्मक्षी तैलहर्ता तिलाकृतिः ॥
verses.transliteration:
viṣa-dātā bhujaṅgaḥ syāt chāgo māṃsasya bhakṣakaḥ |
madhu-hartā bhaven makṣī taila-hartā tilākṛtiḥ ||
verses.translation: A poisoner becomes a serpent; an eater of flesh becomes a goat. A honey-stealer becomes a gadfly; an oil-stealer takes the form of a sesame-cockroach.

verses.hindi:
धान्यहर्ता भवेन्मूषकः फलहर्ता वानरः स्मृतः ।
जलहर्ता चातको जायते तृष्णया पीडितः सदा ॥
verses.transliteration:
dhānya-hartā bhaven mūṣakaḥ phala-hartā vānaraḥ smṛtaḥ |
jala-hartā cātako jāyate tṛṣṇayā pīḍitaḥ sadā ||
verses.translation: A grain-stealer becomes a rat; a fruit-stealer is reborn as a monkey. A water-stealer becomes a chataka bird, ever tormented by thirst.

verses.hindi:
अग्निदाहकरो मूर्खो बकः संजायते ध्रुवम् ।
गृहभेत्ता भवेत् सर्पो देवद्रव्यापहारकः ॥
verses.transliteration:
agni-dāha-karo mūrkho bakaḥ saṃjāyate dhruvam |
gṛha-bhettā bhavet sarpo deva-dravyāpahārakaḥ ||
verses.translation: A foolish arsonist is surely reborn as a crane. A house-breaker becomes a serpent, and so does the stealer of temple property.

verses.hindi:
परापवादकर्ता च मत्स्ययोनौ प्रजायते ।
गुरुनिन्दां करोत्यस्तु शूकरो जायते ध्रुवम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
parāpavāda-kartā ca matsya-yonau prajāyate |
guru-nindāṃ karoty astu śūkaro jāyate dhruvam ||
verses.translation: A slanderer is born in the womb of a fish. He who reviles his teacher is surely born as a pig.

verses.hindi:
मिथ्यासाक्ष्यप्रदो यस्तु मूकं जन्म स विन्दति ।
पङ्क्तिभेदकरो यस्तु जायतेऽन्धोऽतथैव च ॥
verses.transliteration:
mithyā-sākṣya-prado yas tu mūkaṃ janma sa vindati |
paṅkti-bheda-karo yas tu jāyate'ndho'tathaiva ca ||
verses.translation: He who bears false witness is born mute. He who breaks the social order is born blind.

verses.hindi:
गीतवाद्यप्रवादी च क्रौञ्चपक्षी प्रजायते ।
ग्रामयाजी भवेद् गृध्रः कुलटा जायते शुकी ॥
verses.transliteration:
gīta-vādya-pravādī ca krauñca-pakṣī prajāyate |
grāma-yājī bhaved gṛdhraḥ kulaṭā jāyate śukī ||
verses.translation: One who plays music frivolously is reborn as a krauncha bird. A priest who sacrifices for the unworthy becomes a vulture; an unchaste woman becomes a female parrot.

verses.hindi:
प्रतिग्रहरतो विप्रो भेकयोनौ प्रजायते ।
बहुभार्यारतो मर्त्यो बृहत्काये प्रजायते ॥
verses.transliteration:
pratigraha-rato vipro bheka-yonau prajāyate |
bahu-bhāryā-rato martyo bṛhat-kāye prajāyate ||
verses.translation: A Brahmana addicted to accepting gifts is born as a frog. A man devoted to many wives is born in a large-bodied form (as a beast of burden).

verses.hindi:
स्वयं भुञ्जन् न दत्ते यो ह्यतिथिभ्यो न यच्छति ।
स कृमियोनिमापन्नो जायते नात्र संशयः ॥
verses.transliteration:
svayaṃ bhuñjan na datte yo hy atithibhyo na yacchati |
sa kṛmi-yonim āpanno jāyate nātra saṃśayaḥ ||
verses.translation: He who eats by himself and does not give to guests is born in the womb of a worm — there is no doubt about this.

verses.hindi:
वृक्षच्छेदी नरो यस्तु वनच्छेदी विशेषतः ।
शुष्कवृक्षत्वमाप्नोति नगरे निर्जले तथा ॥
verses.transliteration:
vṛkṣa-cchedī naro yas tu vana-cchedī viśeṣataḥ |
śuṣka-vṛkṣatvam āpnoti nagare nirjale tathā ||
verses.translation: A man who cuts trees, especially one who destroys forests, becomes a dry tree in a waterless town.

verses.hindi:
कूपारामतडागादिदेवागारविनाशकः ।
सोऽपि तत्कर्मणा पापी नरके पतति ध्रुवम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
kūpārāma-taḍāgādi-devāgāra-vināśakaḥ |
so'pi tat-karmaṇā pāpī narake patati dhruvam ||
verses.translation: He who destroys wells, gardens, tanks, or temples — that sinner surely falls into hell by that deed.

verses.hindi:
एवं बहुविधा योनिर्लभ्यते पापकर्मभिः ।
तस्मात् सर्वप्रयत्नेन पापं त्यक्त्वा शुभं चरेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
evaṃ bahu-vidhā yonir labhyate pāpa-karmabhiḥ |
tasmāt sarva-prayatnena pāpaṃ tyaktvā śubhaṃ caret ||
verses.translation: Thus, many kinds of wombs are obtained through sinful deeds. Therefore, with every effort, one should abandon sin and perform meritorious acts.

verses.hindi:
दानं तपश्च शौचं च दया सत्यं क्षमा तथा ।
एतानि यस्य वर्तन्ते न स पापेन लिप्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
dānaṃ tapaś ca śaucaṃ ca dayā satyaṃ kṣamā tathā |
etāni yasya vartante na sa pāpena lipyate ||
verses.translation: Charity, austerity, purity, compassion, truthfulness, and forgiveness — he in whom these abide is not stained by sin.

verses.hindi:
श्रीविष्णोश्चरणाम्भोजे यस्य भक्तिर्दृढा भवेत् ।
स मुच्यते महापापैः पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī-Viṣṇoś caraṇāmbhoje yasya bhaktir dṛḍhā bhavet |
sa mucyate mahā-pāpaiḥ padma-patram ivāmbhasā ||
verses.translation: He whose devotion to the lotus feet of Lord Vishnu is firm is freed from great sins, just as a lotus leaf is untouched by water.
